reason: The API-first pivot is valuable, but insufficiently anchored in mandatory domain/state refactoring, increasing risk of implementation-contract divergence under tight delivery pressure.
selected_approach: Device-scoped, contract-first convergence: define a device identity/session control-plane as a domain invariant, publish the dual-device OpenAPI contract first (explicit targeting, concurrency/conflict semantics, defaults), then map existing single-device behavior as the single-device case of this model.
selected_reasoning: This hybrid is strongest because it addresses the core risk in the current codebase assumptions: behavior is currently single-device, so broad refactoring is needed before feature layering. Making device scoping first-class prevents hidden global-state coupling. Making Swagger/OpenAPI the first artifact prevents implementation drift and gives a single compatibility boundary for clients and implementers. reason: Very close to the selected path, but not explicit enough about making the OpenAPI contract the driving migration boundary and migration/deprecation controls.
approach: Device-Scoped Control Plane First: introduce a multi-device domain model and route all new API behavior through it, with current single-device flow as the default device profile.
